title: WebSocket Handshake Request
description: Schema describing the HTTP Upgrade request used to initiate a WebSocket connection as defined in RFC 6455 Section 4.1. The client sends this request to upgrade an HTTP connection to the WebSocket protocol.

method.description: The HTTP method must be GET for the WebSocket opening handshake.

requestUri.description: The Request-URI of the resource being requested, used to identify the WebSocket endpoint.

httpVersion.description: The HTTP version. Must be at least HTTP/1.1.

headers.description: HTTP headers required for the WebSocket opening handshake.

Host.description: The host and optionally the port of the server being connected to.

Upgrade.description: Must be 'websocket' to indicate the client wishes to upgrade to the WebSocket protocol.

Connection.description: Must include 'Upgrade' to signal the connection should be upgraded.

Sec-WebSocket-Key.description: A base64-encoded random 16-byte value used to prove the handshake was received. The server uses this to generate the Sec-WebSocket-Accept response header.

Sec-WebSocket-Version.description: The WebSocket protocol version. Must be 13 as defined in RFC 6455.

Sec-WebSocket-Protocol.description: Optional comma-separated list of subprotocols the client wishes to use, ordered by preference.

Sec-WebSocket-Extensions.description: Optional list of protocol-level extensions the client wishes to use.

Origin.description: The origin of the client making the request. Used by servers to accept or reject cross-origin connections.
